channel.title: Data Breach & Ransomware Aggregator
channel.description: Real-time data breach notifications from state registries, federal sources, and ransomware trackers

item.title: Latin American Cybercriminals Hoover Up Government Data
item.description:
A purported leak exposing 5.8 million records of Uruguayan citizens is the latest incident where cybercriminals targeted government agencies to monetize citizen data.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Ransomware Actors Show Up In Person to Steal Law Firm Data
item.description:
The FBI warned that the extortion gang Silent Ransom Group is targeting law firms and social-engineering its way into servers and databases.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Nordic CISOs Handle Rising Cyber Threats Remarkably Well
item.description:
Artificial intelligence notwithstanding, the vast majority of CISOs in northern Europe say they're facing no more serious cyberattacks than they did two years ago.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Focus on Cyber Insurance: How Quantifying Risk Is Reshaping Security
item.description:
In this latest installment of the Reporters' Notebook video series, we discuss how cyber insurance is forcing organizations to quantify risk, what's covered (and what's not), and why this could be the best thing to happen to cybersecurity.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: As Global Powers Explore Humanoid Robots, Cyber-Risk Looms
item.description:
The future of cybersecurity is germinating, as nation states vie for dominance in the embodied AI market and its supply chain.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 'The Com' Cyberattacks Support Violence & Sexploitation
item.description:
Your organization's security failures have consequences for everyone else too, since this neo-Nazi-infested criminal gang uses its cyber winnings to support more violent and widespread crimes.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
item.description:
Researchers discover an exploit chain combining over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities that could have compromised a popular automation service.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Asia's Cyber Insurance Market Shows Signs of Life
item.description:
The cyber insurance industry has made relatively weak inroads into Asia due to a a variety of factors, but that could be changing.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Name That Toon: Mark of (Cybersecurity) Progress
item.description:
As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary package, we asked readers for a cybersecurity-related caption that captures their thoughts about the industry's last two decades.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: New BTMOB Android Malware Enables Full Device Takeover
item.description:
Delivered via phishing lures, the malware combines financial theft with data exfiltration and remote access. The post New BTMOB Android Malware Enables Full Device Takeover appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Carnival Data Breach Exposed 6 Million People
item.description:
Data breach leaves nearly 6 million Carnival customers navigating identity theft risks. The post Carnival Data Breach Exposed 6 Million People appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Geordie Raises $30 Million for AI Security and Governance Platform
item.description:
The funding round was led by Balderton Capital, with additional support from Crosspoint Capital and previous investors General Catalyst and Ten Eleven Ventures. The post Geordie Raises $30 Million for AI Security and Governance Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Russia-Linked ‘GreyVibe’ Attackers Use AI to Supercharge Cyberattacks
item.description:
Researchers warn GreyVibe’s extensive use of ChatGPT, Gemini, and other AI tools offers a glimpse into how future cybercriminal and state-aligned groups will operate. The post Russia-Linked ‘GreyVibe’ Attackers Use AI to Supercharge Cyberattacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: California Sues 23andMe, Alleging It Failed to Protect User Data in 2023 Breach
item.description:
Attorney General Rob Bonta filed the lawsuit against Chrome Holding Co., which 23andMe rebranded under after filing for bankruptcy last March. The post California Sues 23andMe, Alleging It Failed to Protect User Data in 2023 Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Charter Communications Data Breach Could Impact Nearly 5 Million
item.description:
The notorious ShinyHunters extortion group leaked over 42 million records allegedly stolen from Charter in April. The post Charter Communications Data Breach Could Impact Nearly 5 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: In Other News: Trump Mobile Data Breach, FIFA World Cup Phishing, CISA Responds to Supply Chain Attacks
item.description:
Noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Trump Mobile exposes customer data, phishers target the 2026 FIFA World Cup, CISA responds to recent supply chain attacks. The post In Other News: Trump Mobile Data Breach, FIFA World Cup Phishing, CISA Responds to Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Russia conducting daily attacks on UK 'from seabed to cyberspace,' spy chief warns
item.description:
Anne Keast-Butler, director of GCHQ, said Russia's actions have prompted the agency to defend subsea cables and energy pipelines in British waters, disrupt Russian networks smuggling sanctioned technology and countering “reckless sabotage and assassination attempts.”
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Cruise giant Carnival confirms data breach affecting nearly 6 million people
item.description:
The company said the threat actor gained access to a limited portion of its IT environment last month after compromising an employee account. By the end of April, Carnival determined that the attacker had copied personal information from its systems.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Microsoft calls zero-day releases ‘never justifiable’ as researcher threatens to drop more
item.description:
Each vulnerability was published with working proof-of-concept code to the Microsoft-owned code repository GitHub, making them immediately available to both attackers and security professionals.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
item.description:
Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
item.description:
A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
item.description:
An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description:
Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description:
Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description:
Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Mission Community Hospital Pays $1.55M to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit
item.description:
Deanco Healthcare, LLC, the operator of Mission Community Hospital, an acute care hospital serving patients in the San Fernando Valley […] The post Mission Community Hospital Pays $1.55M to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: OCR Reports to Congress on HIPAA Compliance and Data Breaches in 2024
item.description:
The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) Office for Civil Rights (OCR) has submitted its annual reports to Congress […] The post OCR Reports to Congress on HIPAA Compliance and Data Breaches in 2024 appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: The Oncology Institute Confirms Unauthorized Access to Systems Due to Vendor Breach
item.description:
The Oncology Institute, a publicly traded provider of cancer care through more than 100 clinics in California, Oregon, Nevada, Arizona, […] The post The Oncology Institute Confirms Unauthorized Access to Systems Due to Vendor Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Connecticut Medicaid Portal Breach Affects 22,500 Hartford HealthCare Patients
item.description:
The personal and protected health information of approximately 22,500 Hartford HealthCare patients has been exposed in a security incident. Data […] The post Connecticut Medicaid Portal Breach Affects 22,500 Hartford HealthCare Patients appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Lakeview Health Systems Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit
item.description:
A settlement has been negotiated to resolve a class action lawsuit against Lakeview Health Systems LLC. The lawsuit stemmed from […] The post Lakeview Health Systems Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Healthcare Orgs Lack Confidence in Ability to Defend Against an AI-incited Identity Breach
item.description:
Healthcare organizations have embraced AI and are using AI agents to perform a range of functions, including handling IT support […] The post Healthcare Orgs Lack Confidence in Ability to Defend Against an AI-incited Identity Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: HIPAA Security Rule Training Requirements
item.description:
The HIPAA Security Rule training requirements mandate HIPAA-Covered Entities and HIPAA Business Associates to provide workforce security awareness training that […] The post HIPAA Security Rule Training Requirements appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: California AG Files Lawsuit Over 23andMe Data Breach
item.description:
California Attorney General Rob Bonta has filed a lawsuit against the genetic testing company formerly known as 23andMe over its […] The post California AG Files Lawsuit Over 23andMe Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: PowerSchool’s $17.25 Million Settlement Exposes Years of Student Data Tracking
item.description:
If you ask most people what breach PowerSchool experienced, their first response might be the 2024 hacking incident that affected tens of millions of students. But even before that breach, there was another significant breach involving PowerSchool that began in 2021. Colin Lee and Koji Edmunds report: In early April, many students across the world... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Lithuania investigates theft of 600,000 state registry records
item.description:
Daryna Antoniuk reports: The Lithuanian Prosecutor General’s Office said Friday that attackers gained unauthorized access to more than 600,000 records managed by the Centre of Registers, the state agency responsible for handling property and legal entity records. Prosecutors said the breach involved the misuse of login credentials assigned to institutions authorized to access the databases, and likely... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Malware seller known as “Venom” extradited to France
item.description:
There is an update to an arrest made in Greece in November as part of Operation Endgame. Ekathimerini reports: A 39-year-old Albanian national known online as “Venom” was extradited to France in mid-May after his arrest last November at his apartment in the Nikaia district of Athens. The suspect, who described himself as a construction... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: UK Visa Portal spilled thousands of applicants’ passports and selfies online — and hasn’t fixed the leak
item.description:
Zack Whittaker reports: A website called UK Visa Portal is publicly exposing the passports and selfie photos of applicants who signed up and paid the site to obtain a U.K immigration visa, TechCrunch has learned. An anonymous person notified TechCrunch about the security lapse, saying that the website is exposing at least 100,000 documents from... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Silent Ransom Group Impersonating IT Personnel through Social Engineering
item.description:
The FBI has issued a Flash Alert about the Silent Ransom Group. Summary The Silent Ransom Group (SRG), also known as Luna Moth, Chatty Spider, and UNC3753, is targeting law firms using social engineering techniques. Through phone calls and phishing emails, SRG actors pose as IT support to establish access to victim computers and exfiltrate... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: NL: Schiphol cargo worker arrested over alleged data leaks to drug networks
item.description:
NL Times reports: The Royal Netherlands Marechaussee detained a 24-year-old Amsterdam-based cargo worker at Schiphol on Tuesday, May 19, on suspicion of unauthorized access to computer systems and the leaking of confidential company information, Luchtvaart Nieuws has reported. According to the ongoing investigation, the suspect allegedly used his access to a cargo handling company’s systems at the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Romanian National Sentenced for Selling Access to Networks of Oregon State Government Office
item.description:
A Department of Justice press release on May 27 reports that a Romanian national who faced seven years in prison for selling access to an Oregon state government office in 2021 and other U.S. entities has been sentenced to 56 months in prison: According to court documents, Catalin Dragomir, 46, formerly of Constanta, Romania, sold... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: California AG Bonta Sues Chrome Holding Co., Formerly Known as 23andMe, Over 2023 Data Breach
item.description:
Jaimie Ding reports: Attorney General Rob Bonta filed the lawsuit against Chrome Holding Co., which 23andMe rebranded under after filing for bankruptcy last March. 23andme is known for its direct-to-consumer DNA test kits that provided customers information on their ancestry and genetic predispositions for certain health conditions. The lawsuit calls for various civil penalties against 23andMe... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: French Health Payments Breach Exposed ID Data, Fuels Fraud Fears
item.description:
Michel Gribouille reports: A major French health-care payments middleman says hackers broke into a key authorization portal and may have exposed sensitive personal data, including France’s equivalent of a Social Security number, setting off warnings about identity theft and scam attempts. Almerys, a company that helps process “third-party payment” transactions so patients don’t have to... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Thousands of Oregon prison files accessed by prison worker
item.description:
Noelle Crombie reports on today’s reminder of the insider threat: A former Snake River Correctional Institution employee accessed tens of thousands of Oregon Department of Corrections files over a six-month period last year, the agency announced Friday. Officials discovered the data breach in January during an investigation into misconduct allegations involving the unnamed employee, according to a statement... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: New Gogs zero-day flaw lets hackers get remote code execution
item.description:
An unpatched zero-day vulnerability in the Gogs self-hosted Git service can allow attackers to gain remote code execution (RCE) on Internet-facing instances. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Hackers exploit FortiClient EMS flaw to push infostealer malware
item.description:
Hackers are exploiting an authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-35616) in FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS) to deliver an undocumented credential stealer called EKZ. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: GreyVibe hackers use ChatGPT, Gemini to power cyberattacks
item.description:
A likely Russian threat cluster tracked as GreyVibe has been targeting Ukrainian entities with AI-generated lures and a rich set of custom malware tools. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Charter Communications data breach affects 4.9 million accounts
item.description:
The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information from 4.9 million accounts after hacking the U.S. telecom giant Charter Communications in early April, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: From $5 Attacks to Botnet-Powered Platforms: Inside the DDoS-as-a- Service Market
item.description:
DDoS attacks are increasingly being sold like subscription services, complete with pricing tiers, support, and reseller programs. Flare explores how the DDoS-as-a-Service market has evolved from scattered tools into polished attack platforms. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: California AG sues 23andMe over 2023 breach exposing health data
item.description:
California Attorney General Rob Bonta filed a lawsuit against 23andMe, now Chrome Holding Co., over the company's failure to protect sensitive customer genetic and personal information. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: GS Yuasa Lithium Power Report Victim gsyuasa-lp.com Threat Actor Akira Date Discovered May 29, 2026 Description GS Yuasa Lithium Power is a battery technology and energy storage company â¦May 29, 2026
item.description:
GS Yuasa Lithium Power Report Victim gsyuasa-lp.com Threat Actor Akira Date Discovered May 29, 2026 Description GS Yuasa Lithium Power is a battery technology and energy storage company â¦May 29, 2026 reported a ransomware. Attributed to Everest.
🎭 Threat Actor: Everest | 📰 Source: BreachSense | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: Grupo Premier Report Victim grupopremier.com.mx Threat Actor TheGentlemen Date Discovered May 29, 2026 Description Grupo Premier is an automotive retail and dealership group based â¦May 29, 2026
item.description:
Grupo Premier Report Victim grupopremier.com.mx Threat Actor TheGentlemen Date Discovered May 29, 2026 Description Grupo Premier is an automotive retail and dealership group based â¦May 29, 2026 reported a ransomware. Attributed to Everest.
